channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Law of the jungle — 169 replies — 192.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Law of the jungle

Open thread • Replies: 169 • Updated: 2026-06-16 06:16 UTC

OP

>I am a million billion trillionair-
>BAM BAM BAM
>STAB STAB STAB
W-WHY AM I DYING I AM RICH AND HAVE MONEY ROCKETS CARS AIEEEEEE
P-PLEASE SIRE SAVE M-
>BAM BAM BAM
NOOOO WHY IS MONEY USELESS WHY WHY WHY WAAAAH PLEASE SIR
I WILL GIVE YOU EVERYTHIN-
>STAB HIS KIDS
AAAAAAH MY KIDDIES MY BLOODLINE WHY WHY
PLEASE SIR THE TRILLION MONEY
ACK

Early replies (first 3)

62359354
>>62359336
a mentally buck busted normie is going to say """the law""" grants the rich physical immunity.... and even if it doesn't, they have like, body guards who are omnipotent, omniscient, and omnipresent. you can't do ANYTHING to the rich!!!! it's literally impossible!!!!!

62359360
>>62359336
We live in capitalism and I'm protected by the pigs u need to learn how to move nigga

62359387
>nigger tier envy thread
>/biz/

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62370741
>>62370434
how do you think our current society is upheld?

62370770
Lmao actual cryptokikes and kikes defending the rich

62370820
>>62361047
>rich
>intelligence
Only room in your bag for one, I'm afraid.

62370830
>>62362126
>let the smart people do the things that make society go
This is what speculators actually believe.

62371364
>>62370741
>upheld
It....isn't?
We had a wildly successful capitalistic system in the 'west' for the last 300 years, and a facsimile of one for the last century (thanks smoke and mirrors fiat), with increasingly onerous compliance demands by the state that start to decouple free markets toward state operated markets (which is far closer to the current paradigm). State regulated markets fucking suck and are a constantly changing landscape of chosen winners and losers based on which group is in power at any one time. Markets are wavering and unstable, currencies are starting to decouple, and global stability is starting to crack under the weight of unnatural (government mandated) migration and capital flows.
Don't ask disingenuous questions, for they open a two way window into your mind (which isn't a compliment)

62371408
>>62359336
thats why we gate keep niggers like that away from us via invitation only or simply not walking near them or anywhere around them your response? ever see batman?

62371411
>>62359336
im basically batman

62371435
>>62365552
I made it. From living in a 1975 Ford maverick to having land and houses and a wife and 3 kids. Go fucking jump, you're a useless eater and only a burden to the rest of us. Fucking pathetic faggot.

62371440
>>62371408
Yeah stay in your house Timmy
Don't let us see you in any cities or public areas

62371441
>>62365566
More than you, pal.

62371449
>>62371435
I will kill all of them and molest their corpses. I'll send you a DVD

62371454
>>62371441
you didn't answer the question. >>62360783

62371459
>>62371449
YOU
WONT
DO
SHIT

62371463
>>62371440
at least i have a house

62371468
>>62371459
I'm tracing your IP as we speak. Prepare for the storm that is coming, maggot

62371472
watch out yall the "people" are trying to uhhhhhhhhh idk

62372199
i stand undefeated

62372328
>>62372199
>undefeated
Then you are clearly not mr john communism

62373377
>>62371463
Slave mentality. If you are not born upper class you'll never be more than a piece of cattle to them. To be extracted, abused, slaughtered, and thrown onto the rubbish pile of the forgotten.
The only way to regain your honour and dignity is by fighting them to the death and replacing their ranks entirely with your own.
They like to claim that they in their positions because they're smarter and more capable. They are not. They are more ruthless, more violent and that's all it ever was!

62373388
We have nothing to lose but our chains! What do we have else? Our lives? Life will pass before you know it, make it count you cowards!

62373610
class cucks went real quiet

62373692
>>62365676
>lectures about being a sociopath for buying and selling stuff
>unironically aspires to murder strangers in cold blood out of envy
your brain on communism

62373696
>>62373692
welcome to the real world, pussy
Those "strangers" are vampires drunk on my blood and the blood of my brothers and my ancestors

62373718
>>62373696
Nothing "real world" about uttering two antithetical ideas in the same breath. It also doesn't make you tough. Just retarded.

62373727
>>62373718
I hope you know what happens to class traitors :)

62373746
>>62373727
>whining on 4chan since 2007
>but now his depressed drunken tough talk is totez 4 realz
you won't do shit

62373751
>>62373746
and you're too pussy to even talk shit about your masters

62373760
i suddenly feel very horny

62373773
>>62373751
>toothless impotent rage makes me a badass!
kek

62374025
>>62373773
>being le stoic cuck makes me better

item.title: OMGOMGOMGOMG IT HASNT DUMPED WITHIN THE first FULL DAY OF TRADING ITS GOING UP FOREVER AND ALWAYS NO BRAKES ON THIS TRAI… — 25 replies — 100.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
OMGOMGOMGOMG IT HASNT DUMPED WITHIN THE first FULL DAY OF TRADING ITS GOING UP FOREVER AND ALWAYS NO BRAKES ON THIS TRAI…

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-06-16 06:59 UTC

OP

OMGOMGOMGOMG IT HASNT DUMPED WITHIN THE first FULL DAY OF TRADING ITS GOING UP FOREVER AND ALWAYS NO BRAKES ON THIS TRAIN UP ONLY FOREVER!!! MONEY ISNT REAL. ROCKETS ARE!!!!
>1 TRILLION mARKET CAP
>2 TRILLION MARPET KAP
>U R HER
>3 TRILLIONAIRES
>10 TRILIONIES
>100 TRILLIONZES

Early replies (first 3)

62372684
Strap in, we're going to the moon.

62372691
>>62372673
Can anyone tell me exactly what space x is supposed to be doing?
Mining on the moon?

62372703
>>62372691
I can't believe this needs to be explained but the answer is AI. They said they will build data centers in space and that's all that is needed.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62372710
>>62372691
Mining on mars with robots. No regulatory agencies have the technology to regulate on mars so the sheer number of resources that can be obtained will be substantial. The moon is to be a launchpad.

62372747
>>62372703
>>62372710
>data centers in space
>robot mining on Mars
Amazing, does that happen before or after we get fully autonomous self-driving teslas? Maybe he'll build a hyperloop, but for rockets - that way the minerals are transported faster. I'm so heckin excited guys

62372748
>>62372673
He was right, SpaceX really is driving the goyim insane. They can't handle it and are making threads like this now.

62372768
>>62372691

62372772
>>62372691
lining the pockets of institutional investors

62372779
>>62372747
It looks like famine is next. Anyhoo, the datacenter craze is just a thing to allocate land and utilities to future projects. Most of those won't actually be data centers. A lot of them are going to be neocities. Others to be massive industrial plants. It's just this way they can be allocated funds and space without having to go into details about their data center that isn't a data center project. One of the jokes is that the emissions and usage data used to smear data centers are actually just city data. So it's people arguing against having cities because of the environmental impact.

62372794
>>62372691
SpaceX(and all other X companies which Elon wants to eventually merge together) is trying to become become the irl Wayland Yutani, excluding all the biotech/synth/ayylmao stuff

62372825
>>62372794
>excluding all the biotech/synth/ayylmao
It'll definitely include biotech and synths (neuralink and XAI)

62372894
200, 300 next losers lmao

62372951
>>62372691
>Can anyone tell me exactly what space x is supposed to be doing?
printing infinite money, apparently

>Amazing, does that happen before or after we get fully autonomous self-driving teslas? Maybe he'll build a hyperloop, but for rockets - that way the minerals are transported faster. I'm so heckin excited guys
lamao
maybe he can first make a non-crap car (here on Earth)

62373030
>>62372673
Honestly it could go to $1k. Why? Because only 5% of the shares are being traded. It's a trillion dollar market cap but only trading $100 billion worth of shares.
It will only be unsustainable for companies to pump it when they start unlocking the smelly 20% and 28% investor lockups.
The first days when only 5% of shares are traded will be the most profitable for companies to scam pump and pin the price higher because it's easier to do it.

62373088
>>62372673
this but unironically

62373570
>>62372673
Correct.

62373577
How do I short this?

62373893
>>62372684

62373947
Goyim are literally going to rope en masse when it doesn’t crash

62373982
>>62372673
Elon will be a quadrillionaire EOW
Screencap this

62374014
>>62372691
Does it matter. The stock market is all rigged, none of it makes sense. Learn to read the tea-leaves and do the best you can

62374065
are we still dealing in the real world, fellow goyim?

62374074
>>62372673
Haha i recognize that frustration from trying to short TSLA ages ago. Just accept that the market is phony balony, dont do the rational thing just go along with trends regardless of how regarded it is

62374084
>62374074
nice b8 m8

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 19 replies — 76.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-06-16 07:01 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62373803
>>62373772
You are posting anime. NGMI.

62373818
>>62373772
>>62373799
>>62373803
I hope you all have less than $10k

62373831
>tripfag is a brokie

Color me surprised.

62373838
>>62373818
I am 47 and I make $165k a year. Seethe.

62373864
>>62373838
Funny, I'm 27. A 20 year gap. Wouldn't mind being 17 again.

62373866
>>62373803
Posting animu has been a very effective method to gatekeep and single out posters which do not belong. You need to go back.
>>62373798
I wanted to propose pic related. Her name's in the filename and is practically a dead meme from an era long past. We already have pink wojaks so there's already some cross pollination plus there's a good chunk of her content being collected in a booru.

62373871
>>62373866
Okay, bro. Go gatekeep /reddit

62373881
>>62373752
I made the six figure club with stocks and crypto. Lost most of it. Now I’m trying to make it again and more with just stocks after a long hiatus. I realized I’ve been over diversifying. Growing a small account fast will require fewer but larger bets. And patience for a good entry.

62373888
>>62373866
Okay
First order of business settled.

Next order of business.

$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$

Let's brainstorm. I think we should find something to learn. This thing can't be too mainstream, can't be too complex for our brains, and must be fun enough to encourage people to come back to these threads. We shall do this [subject] for x amount of threads and if NOTHING substantial comes from it, we move on to a new [subject]

62373902
>>62373888
I've seen a couple of drawfags post on /biz/ with a pressing question about how to monetize their art. What I'm about to suggest will definitely delete these threads because it involves a venture which for whatever reason jannies/mods will see it as "advertising" some personal project. If you're serious about starting a fun /biz/ related hobby we should brainstorm elsewhere without interference.

62373918
>>62373902
Will you stop crying about jannies??????

I will go ahead and do the brainstorming since you're terrified of the meek. Your cowardice is really annoying. Like you're begging them to delete it, first post in this thread and it's you putting ideas into the heads of jannies.

Give me one moment while I gather my thoughts, it might take a few minutes as I'd like to lay out multiple ventures that will appeal to a wide amount of sensibilities and then we will converge on one.

62373951
I have to wage in the morning.
It's too much to ask for this thread to last until then.
So for now, we have accomplished one thing.

I shall go to bed and tomorrow we will begin discussing various money making opportunities. I will try to put myself into as many different shoes as I can. Can millions be made? Unlikely. Can $1 be made? VERY likely. Goodnight anons.

62373961
>>62373951
Yeah it's about that time for me too, but I still think we should really start a group elsewhere.

62374042
>>62373866
>>62373881
>>62373888
>>62373918
>>62373951
>>62373961

Kys’selves anime fags

62374054
>>62373752
>devise ways to gain a spot at the table
DCA crypto. It's literally your only hope. Good luck.

62374086
>>62373752
>In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.
I'm in the six figure club. Six figure salary, six figure portfolio. There is no six figure club, six figures isn't enough to do anything, your life doesn't change. You still have to wageslave like any other poorfag.

item.title: BREAKING NEWS: RIPPLE PRIME LAUNCH JULY 2026 — 19 replies — 53.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
BREAKING NEWS: RIPPLE PRIME LAUNCH JULY 2026

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-06-16 06:47 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62368976
>>62368970
The next leg down will be more brutal than i expected, maybe 1100$ eth isnt out of question.

62368977
>>62368970
What does this have to do with xrp? Not very boomish, very bearish.

62368989
>BOOOOOOOM! * * *

216 KB
BREAKING NEWS: RIPPLE PRIME LAUNCH JULY 2026 Anonymous (ID: t115BMRU) 06/15/26(Mon)01:55:29 No.62368970>>62368976 >>62368977

Anonymous (ID: 8Yslbhfo) 06/15/26(Mon)01:59:07 No.62368976

>>62368970 (OP)
The next leg down will be more brutal than i expected, maybe 1100$ eth isnt out of question.

Anonymous (ID: lkO8Bpk1) 06/15/26(Mon)01:59:41 No.62368977

>>62368970 (OP)
What does this have to do with xrp? Not very boomish, very bearish.

Reply to Thread
[Index] [Catalog] [Top] Update
[Advertise on 4chan]
Delete Post: [File Only] Style:

About • Feedback • Legal • Contact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62368995
KAAAAAAABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM
>boolish ecksarepee

62369060
Wow pretty bullish (xrp)

62369062
>>62368977
It barely has anything to do with Ripple, let alone XRP kek
DTCC services literally the entire US financial market, and Ripple bought a tradfi company.
That's the connection.

BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOMMMMMM

62369092
>>62369062
>It barely has anything to do with Ripple
>Ripple Prime
>The XRP blockchain can support early settlement
within a Digital Prime Brokerage framework by
enabling onchain credit lines that fund settlement
ahead of the standard net settlement cycle, with
funding costs applied explicitly and transparently

62369109
>>62369092
Ripple Prime is just the renamed tradfi company "Hidden Road".
Ripple bought it, and it hasn't changed its operations, it's still a tradfi company.

And pretty much all tradfi companies in the US have something to do with DTCC.

62369234
>>62369109
>Ripple Prime is just the renamed tradfi company "Hidden Road".
Correct
>Ripple bought it, and it hasn't changed its operations, it's still a tradfi company.
Incorrect, they integrated the XRP ledger. As per their own whitepaper https://ripple.com/reports/the-blueprint-for-institutional-digital-assets-trading.pdf
>And pretty much all tradfi companies in the US have something to do with DTCC.
Its about the tokenization service that aims to bridge TradFI and Defi.

How much link do you hold?

62369256
>>62369234
>they integrated the XRP ledger. As per their own whitepaper https://ripple.com/reports/the-blueprint-for-institutional-digital-assets-trading.pdf
ctrl+f "XRP ledger": 0 results
ctrl+f "XRPL": 0 results

And even if that were true, it means Ripple Prime integrated XRPL; it has nothing to do with DTCC beyond that.

>Its about the tokenization service that aims to bridge TradFI and Defi.
Ripple Prime isn't a "tokenization service".

62369283
>>62369256
Its literally copy pasted in my first post.
The XRP blockchain can support early settlement within a Digital Prime Brokerage framework by enabling onchain credit lines that fund settlement ahead of the standard net settlement cycle, with funding costs applied explicitly and transparently

>>62369256
>it has nothing to do with DTCC beyond that.
The OPs picture has TEXT in it. https://www.dtcc.com/news/2026/may/04/dtcc-advances-development-of-new-tokenization-service

>Ripple Prime isn't a "tokenization service".
I never said that. OPs picture states that its the DTCs tokenization service and ripple prime is involved.

Its getting silly at this point.

62369291
>>62369283
>The XRP blockchain can support early settlement within a Digital Prime Brokerage framework
It "CAN" support that, yes.
Any blockchain can.

Where's the proof that Ripple Prime integrated XRPL though?

>OPs picture states that its the DTCs tokenization service and ripple prime is involved.
Everything DTCC touches is "involved" in their tokenization efforts.
And DTCC touches pretty much every financial institution in the world in some way.

62369363
>>62369291
Anybody can mint a token on the xrpl and do whatever they want with it.

62369376
>>62369363
You can mint tokens on pretty much any chain. And on chains with smart contracts you can do a lot more with them too. Which is why nobody used XRPL to mint tokens in 14 years. It's also why Ripple made an EVM chain that is completely separate from XRPL

62370203
>>62368970
KAABBBLLLAAAAAGHHHHHHH

62370912
>>62368970
BOOOOOOOM

62373891
>>62369256
>>62369291
>>62369062
>>62369109
Ripple Prime is a broker dealer that clears trillions of dollars annually with a specialty in digital assets. It is a direct DTCC participant, with very few peers and none like it vis-a-vis crypto network specificity. You are wrong and gay. Cope.
https://ripple.com/ripple-press/ripple-acquires-prime-broker-hidden-road/

62374059
>>62373891
Even if Hidden Road/Ripple Prime inherently used XRPL (which it doesn't), that has nothing to do with DTCC other than Ripple Prime in turn uses DTCC.

How fucking retarded are you, really.

62374061
>>62373891
Bullish gish gallop

item.title: How do I fully Divest from the USA — 47 replies — 32.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
How do I fully Divest from the USA

Open thread • Replies: 47 • Updated: 2026-06-16 05:42 UTC

OP

Anyone who’s still investing in U.S markets at this point is abetting fascism. I want a total European portfolio to support Liberal democracy

Early replies (first 3)

62368148
I unironically do this and it's easy.

Just a case of EU+UK tracker ETFs + Canada and Australia + Japan-Taiwan-SK. I balance it out with pan-European IT and defence ETFs to balance out the overabundance of financials and industrials.

This gives you a portfolio of liberal democracies.

62368154
>>62368132
>fascism

62368162
>>62368154
You’re just uneducated on history

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62369190
>>62368132
What's PDA?

62369208
>>62369190
Having sex at the workplace

62369243
>>62368216
>dictatorial
>theocracy
>rounded up in camps and killed
GENUINELY what the fuck are you referring to?

62369274
>>62369243
NTA, but sounds like he is talking about Project 2025.

62370119
You shouldnt divest away from strategic US sectors. If the US falls, the last thing on your mind will be your portfolio, so an investment in the US is an investment into the future.

62370160
>>62368132
>fighting for their rights to perform PDA at work
Lmao for fucks sake man... Euros are such a bunch of degenerate faggots

62370630
>>62368132
Whatever companies you buy, you have to make sure they dont use any products from sp500 comapnies. Good luck!

62370666
>>62368132
Only if you hate money. Even European defense companies are down this year. America and the AI companies are just dominating everything.

America is evil but Satan and his jews rule this world and have ALL the money.

62370699
I hope no one ITT lets his investments be influenced by posts containing the words "Jewsa", "pozzed", "Jewmericans" or "Satan"

62370702
>>62368132
>this is the difference between us
having creeps in your workplace?

62370714
Its impossible, even if you only invest in thirdie companies their biggest customer is burger. Even if you only invest in sanctioned countries they use third parties to sell to burgers

62370718
>>62370714
Tl;dr - the great satan wins

62370721
>>62370160
It's just gonna be bunch of gays "expressing" themselves.

62370725
>>62368132
Have you not learnt to never bet against America?

62370726
>>62370630
>>62370160
>>62370119

>>62368132
Isn't Spain giving workers breaks to masturbate?
Kekaroonies.

62370733
I wonder if there are people as retarded as OP but unironically.
I'm glad I sold all my EU crap at a + because this shit is going up in flames.

62370754
This place is fucking dying and we are being ruled over by burectautic women and boomers stuck in yesterday. Do not put money in here. This place will be a testaments what happens when retarded libs take over and encourage societies to rot in the name of progressivism

62370778
>>62368148
Thanks bro, i’ve been wondering how to invest so that my investments both generate money and strenghten european enlightenment.

62370787
>>62368279
UK is more like USA than like rest of europe.

62370799
>>62368999
Enjoy sucking Putin’s dick like your country already did for many years, retard. It’s either the union or sucking either Trump’s or Putin’s dick.

62370817
>>62368132
But what if i want to abet fascism?

62370824
>>62370754
I wonder when the first big cunt takes a look at Germany's raw numbers from 2022 onward and nopes the fuck out. And the EU is just Germany / German money on a proxy.

62370828
>>62370725
>>62370666
>>62370119
Kek americans coping hard here. You guys got lucky with the world wars but just because you got lucky one doesn’t mean you will keep outperforming everything forever. Everyone tells you to keep buying at the top.

62370935
>>62368132
Listen here you capitalistic chud. In Europe we DO NOT invest, that’s for capitalist American pigs. What you MUST do with your left over money is hand it over to the brave and hardworking souls from North Africa who need our help now more than ever. We OWE them and it’s YOUR personal responsibility to be held accountable for the actions of your ancestors, and provide them with proper care and a bright livelihood. So go donate as much all your left over money that you don’t even need, only a facist scumbag would do otherwise.

62370975
>>62370935
>account based in indorussia

62370977
>>62368132
lol

62370980
>>62370754
The wealthy men of today are a bunch of dysgenic narcissists as well, so with the world having no means of justice in this generation, evacuating the entire North American continent and sending everybody back to where they came from is starting to look like a relatively appealing outcome at this point. It would at least establish how completely unacceptable this society was and that its history should never repeat again.

62370981
>>62368132

62373787
>>62370787
Sweden is the America of Europe. Except if the gayest commie New England Democrats had total power for 100 years.

>>62368132

item.title: No regrets selling BTC?? — 102 replies — 31.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
No regrets selling BTC??

Open thread • Replies: 102 • Updated: 2026-06-16 03:56 UTC

OP

Some talk about regretting it.

Anyone sold and still thinks it was the right move?

If you had the cash from that sale sitting in your account today, would you buy BTC again? Because I definitely would.

Early replies (first 3)

62339349
I sold my last bitcoin last year. Shitcoin is dead. AI is dead. Time to start acting like an african american.

62339372
>>62339349
Maybe regrets for buying? Should have bought chainlink

62339382
I rotated into XMR at like $105k, no regrets at all.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62355617
>>62355133
It's crazy that DeFi security is still one of the biggest problems in the space.

62355631
>>62354827
Buy avax today and sell @ $100

62355632
>>62339417
Monero programmers, like the telegram founder, can be compromised
And monero is based on bitcoin. The entire crypto market exists because of all the money in bitcoin which is now backed by the US government.

62356269
>>62355617
At this point, thorough audits and multiple layers of review should be the standard, not a nice bonus anon.

62356538
>>62355631
What's your basis?

62357046
>>62345600
Probably the better route. We've seen too many centralized services blow up over the years, so you can't really blame people for preferring fully decentralized setups.

62357057
>>62355632
What about ZEC?

62357077
ICP

62357115
>>62339328
I sold at 116k I dont regret it at all and have about 2 mil waiting for sept-dec buy in.

62357126
>>62339328
I can never regret buying BTC desu, I bought back in around 70K and will DCA more if price goes below 60K. I'm also staking a little BTC on babylon for some extra yield

62357923
>>62355632
>Monero programmers, like the telegram founder, can be compromised
yeah just like the bitcoin developers right? they're the ones who control bitcoin?

it's a good question though, what if they were? miners would have to agree on consensus on which code to accept. if github ever refused to host monero's code then it would just move to .onion or .i2p

62359525
>>62357046
That's how it goes. They've already got GoMining, Ledger, and a16z involved, so let's see who they bring in next.

62359541
>>62357057
ZEC is interesting. If the privacy narrative picks up again, it's usually one of the first names people bring up.

62359557
>>62339328
I literally don't care about it. Mogged by virtually everything else over the last couple of years.

Also why is faggot shit like dogecoin still worth billions of dollars.

>in b4 bottom signal
Probably.

62359588
>>62357126
Sounds like you've got a plan and you're sticking to it. Hard to ask for much more than that. I wouldn't be surprised if more people follow suit over time. The $4B+ TVL shows there's already plenty of interest.

62359608
>>62359557
The market has a weird habit of rewarding the things nobody can rationally explain.

62359673
>>62357077
People still remember the launch, but the tech discussion around ICP is often more interesting than the price chart.

62360479
>>62359557
When bottom

62361782
>>62360479
Nobody knows

62362657
>>62359557
How do you mean?

62362661
>>62339328
While BTC is in deep bear stage, it's time to get in. I need to be greedy when others are fear.

62363445
>>62362657
It's his opinion

62364599
>>62339349
>Time to start acting like an african american.
finally someone said it

62365449
>>62362661
You're just doing the right thing..

62365460
any time you can cash out, turn a profit and not get anally blasted by exchanges is a good time to cash out.

62368338
>>62364599
Damn

62369180
Obviously my >$100k sells were the "right move". Cycle theory helped a lot with that one. If I had the clarity of hindsight, I would have sold more. Bitcoin is amazing tech but its potential is as-yet unrealized. Holding it doesn't do much for anyone by itself. Sure, people say you can borrow against it or maybe get yield somewhere - fuck that shit. I need to see a thriving system of exchange based on Bitcoin replacing the current economy before I keep it around for practical reasons.
Until that point, I trade dollars for Bitcoin, Bitcoin for more dollars, and dollars for more Bitcoin. How I trade it is based on the likelihood of reaching that final evolutionary stage, and right now it's looking pretty dire. What the establishment wants is to turn Bitcoin into a exotic monetary instrument held for bragging points and dick measurement reasons, and that's what people like Saylor and ETFs are doing, and why OGs are dumping on their faces. What I want to see more of is trustless stateless peer-to-peer transactions involving real shit like commodities, cars, houses etc. which seems to be happening through Monero.

Anyways I'm hoping to buy back lower around August-September. Maybe $35-40k. Thanks for reading my diary.

62371627
>>62365460
time will tell.

62373525
>>62345783
Depends on your preference tho

62373683
>>62359588
>>62359608
>>62359673
>>62361782
>>62363445
>>62365449
>>62368338
>>62371627
>>62373525
SHUT THE ACTUAL FUCK UP

item.title: how much money do u need to date women? Also are they ok if u are not ambitious career driven like if i have 10M but NEE… — 56 replies — 22.5/hr
item.content:encoded:

Open thread • Replies: 56 • Updated: 2026-06-16 04:39 UTC

OP

how much money do u need to date women? Also are they ok if u are not ambitious career driven like if i have 10M but NEET would they be fine with it.. probably not i think they want you to be grinding your job 24/7 and making like 400k a year

Early replies (first 3)

62365550
dating is outdated chud, find something else to focus on

62365555
>>62365550
but i want a gf to watch mobis with, to eat some delicious food, cook together maybe and stuff.. I got a bluray player recently and im building up a collection we could do it together if i had a gf

:(

62365557
>>62365540
a good prostitute in euro cuck land will service you starting from 100$ for 30 mins. Anything else is cope

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62366027
>>62365989

>become her bf
>she cheats on you with a nigga that has NOTHING of this

62366041
>>62365608
Animal should be allowed to just breed and leave, there is nothing like third parties in the interaction.

62366052
>>62365557
>dude just get herpes with a romanian gypsy in a shitty hotel in the suburbs

kys

62366054
>Women or leveraged, margined silver miners?
Mmmmm...... you're gonna have to get back to me on that bro

62366257
>>62365587
>>62365738
>>62366005
^ This system is outdated, but I've invented a new one. The equation is identical, but the compounding effects of the numerator have been upscaled, personality remains the same. Proximity now has a negative and positive value.

62366581
>>62366052
Yep, sounds incredibly based

62366590
>>62365989
Delusion
Just look at families walking around

62366599
>>62365958
>>62365953
Do you know any married men
And those things can be deal breakers, there are lines/limits

62366630
>>62365540
the only girls that care about careers in 2026 are egg freezing hags. you need just enough money to get to where women are (like the bar or anywhere else) and live somewhere. anything past that is diminishing returns.

62366635
anon don't listen to desperate insecure virgins for dating advice
the world is full of poor women who will date you if you have a good personality
when my wife and I started dating, I was literally unemployed and living with my parents
Now I make more than her but it took me 5 years to get there.

62366639
>>62365555
>I got a bluray player recently and im building up a collection

Dad?

62366677
>>62365608
all of these women made a different man spend thousands and wait months before fucking really makes you ponder

62366728
>>62366599
well eventually these kinds of women also tend to be single mothers

62367007
>>62365897
Yes paradoxically the girls that are most wife material aren't the ones going around looking to date. I think if you are out of school you're out of luck
>Girlfriend through connections
I know this sounds stupid, but I'd feel like a cuck if I met my girlfriend through a mutual.
Think about it, MY girlfriend would have been interested in talking to someone else FIRST. I wouldn't want to date a girl with guy friends anyways

Impossible propositions are abound

62367017
>>62366635
Do the math.

62367050
>>62367007
Yeah, that’s pretty retarded.
There are plenty of non-sexual reasons why a guy might know some other girl, like a family member, girlfriend/wife’s friend, former co-worker, former school friend.
You could make friends with girls to try to date one of their friends too.
The crux is on your ability to be a normal person to any normal person regardless of gender or age.
That’s how you find girls who aren’t sluts and divorce rapists. Not how you sleep around though.
So you have to figure out if what you really want is someone to have sex with regularly, or someone to start a family with. Because it’s a lot of social effort.

62367055
>>62367007
I just realized, you may have severely low self esteem if you can’t handle the thought of a gf talking to someone else before you.
Low self esteem is girl repellant
Fix that first

62367080
>>62365555
Checked and damn feels bad, I hope you make it.

>>62365540
Former sex haver here, if she loves you not much. Unironically you could go have a picnic with some bullshit like pizza, or go on a road trip. Strategic use of money is ideal. Don't just buy flowers, get them delivered to her work so all her coworkers get pissed and jealous. You want enough money to lock down a nice wedding and kids but thats really it, this was my fatal mistake. I have enough for three weddings now but opportunities are gone for me.

62367870
I get 1k CAD neetbux and do just fine
If not a demo thread, you're overthinking it retard

62367927
>>62365555
unironically, where are the lazy NEET gfs?

62368381
>>62365555
I just want to watch gun boat ball 2 wit my aspie unicorn believing gf turned housewife mommy with fear of scrutiny. I guess i was born too late

62368990
>>62367050
>>62367055
Think about it though. If a girl has tons of guy friends, that's a pretty clear no signal right?
>Low self esteem
true :(

62369133
>>62368990
>>62368990
You can make friends with women too anon. Women that you aren't interested in can have friends that you are interested in.

Also girls can have guy friends that they don't wanna fuck.

Unironically truely befriend a lesbian, this will teach you how to act around women without acting like a retard as you won't try to fuck her.

Also if you're so insecure talk to a therapist/take shrooms and start working out. Its all ego that you have to let go in the end.

Good luck fren!

62369150
All the matches I got on tinder is 30s post wall roast, I can’t believe the 28yi tinder matches something literally call me old hag when I’m just a 35yo bald, obese, needy, short, no STEM degree, and potentially impotent.

62369226
>>62365540

62369807
>>62365540

62371591
>>62369133

>>62368990
You’re letting your insecurities delude you.

62371912
>>62365555
you sound like a retard

62371972
>>62369133
>Also girls can have guy friends that they don't wanna fuck.
even if this is the case the guy most likely wants to fuck them you retard.

part of being in a relationship is having a basic level of respect for your partner. that means dropping all single behaviour, so no hanging out 1 on 1 with the opposite sex, no texting other guys, and no getting drunk without you in bars/clubs. its not insecurity to expect a the most basic of relationship boundaries.

>just take drugs bro!
>go pay money to a millenial woman
holy bluepilled retard

62373795
>>62369807
tbf net worth is not how much money you have

item.title: Memes aside, listen to macro doomers like Lyn Alden, you'll be much poorer — 8 replies — 21.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Memes aside, listen to macro doomers like Lyn Alden, you'll be much poorer

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-16 06:46 UTC

OP

She's the second coming of Peter Schiff.
People listened to him after the GFC, after he got famous for
>being right once in a row
and his investors (or those who just followed his advice) went heavily into metals, commodities, emerging markets, and European (WTF?) markets, and missed out on the most epic US equity bull run.
Then after a decade of leading investors astray, Schiff sold Europacific Capital and "retired" to Puerto Rico, where he spends his days on the beach and his nights fucking chicas.

Early replies (first 3)

62372855
spame more threads fuddie

62372860
>>62372844
>"retired" to Puerto Rico, where he spends his days on the beach and his nights fucking chicas.
Another macro doomer my dad used to listen to named Harry S. Dent did the same thing, BTW.
No income tax, and plenty of poor, desperate chicas willing to sell their bodies to older, wealthy white stateside-expats for security.

>>62372844

item.title: Questions from a Mid-20’s Investor — 33 replies — 11.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Questions from a Mid-20’s Investor

Open thread • Replies: 33 • Updated: 2026-06-16 04:22 UTC

OP

Hello /biz/ I will try and keep this brief
Currently I have been investing most of my take home pay simply into VOO as I only just recently started investing
It seemed simple, solid mid-long term option, and sensical for starting out to not fuck up while getting exposure
Now, this has nothing to do with FOMO, but I’m wondering if I should start aiming towards investing in either specific sector ETFs or maybe specific stocks to chase higher returns
My portfolio is currently at $60K mostly in VOO, perhaps with enough DCA I can reach $100k by years end or at least close to that, depends on growth
Currently my total portfolio return ranges from 17-20% depending on the month for context
I’ve seen recently how well things like SOXX have been doing and was considering finding a sector ETF or two to go into since I already have so much broad market exposure
To be clear, this is not me chasing returns based off past performance, I am well aware growth has to come from somewhere and is not infinite so I’m considering CAGRs as far back as possible
With all this in mind, any recommendations? Find a good sector ETF, go into a specific stock, or maybe just focus on getting more income first and then continue to just VOO and chill
And no I don’t wanna sell options doing covered calls or buy your shit coin etc etc
I should add the sectors I’m interested in are semi-conductors, energy (ones that deal with technology needed to cool data centers), and perhaps drones / nuclear for commercial and military usage

Early replies (first 3)

62369867
>>62369835
XLK
SOXL

62369880
>>62369835
Recreate the ETF so you avoid paying management fees.. Not super relevant for VOO, but if you go into niche sector specific ETFs, the management fees are usually over 0.5%

62370000
>>62369867
Not sure how I feel about the triple leverage with SOXL can you red pill me on it, it’s the same deal as QQQ vs TQQQ or whatever the hell it’s called right
But otherwise you’re just advising do some tech and semiconductor either way
>>62369880
Fair point I’ll consider that

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62370048
>>62369835
I need a young emo woman with big titties that loves me unconditionally, fugg. but I'm afraid of intimacy and I have a lot of insecurities about my height, dick size, body odours and I don't know what love is. I have deep appreciation for my family and loved ones but I don't really love them. I'm too schizoid. I miss the touch of a girl

62370061
>>62370048
Did Pomni actually say this?

62370063
>>62370048
Have you tried the Philippines or Belarus?

62370075
>>62370048
>I don't know what love is
its a fantasy concept like magic unironically

62370087
>>62370061
it was in the DVD director commentaries audiotrack
>>62370075
I suspect this as much. I will never live a "normal" life because of it, though

62370103
>>62370048
You need to study hoemath's content thoroughly and if possible invest the 30 bucks into his self max product. I have been watching his live sessions for the past week and boooy oh boy did they open my eyes to the mistakes i was making. Anyways you either work on yourself or drift aimlessly not getting what you want and nees so its up to you big guy

62370125
Yeah looking into it more I think I’ll start DCA a bit into SOXX, if it eventually goes down I know it’ll rebound

62370258
>>62370103
Gee these bots sure are fast to shill paid ads

62370367
Stop talking about Foids please

62370622
>>62369835
>Currently I have been investing most of my take home pay simply into VOO
Just keep doing that anon. You will have better gains than 90% of the anons here.

>>62370048
I had a 20 year old gf a few years ago with g cups and i didnt appreciate tits back then.
I miss this shit now like you wouldnt believe, there are literally 0 women with that big tits without being a lardass out there. I didnt knew what i had bros...

>>62370367
Fuck off. A nice big tiddy gf is very important if you want to be a better trader/investor.

62370823
>>62370622
Fair enough

62370996
>>62370622
Also I only care about Japanese breasts

62371007
>>62370996
You're financially ok with brown nipples?

62371211
>>62371007
A lot are pink
Honestly I’ve never seen a brown nipple before

62371239
>>62371211
pink nip /= nip
anime is not real life chud.
jap girls have brown ones irl

62371250
>>62369835
Honestly man, the amount of effort it takes to beat the SP500 (or NASDAQ, which is even a bit better recently and imo long term from now on) is relatively high. You see countless examples of lifelong financial/investing experts who would have done better if they just did nothing but buy the SP500 and sit on it.

I'm invested more heavily into semiconductors and AI stocks but that's because I have industry knowledge. Industry knowledge can help you determine what is and is not good for long term investing. But then again, I also have industry knowledge in medicine and I won't touch biotech/pharma stocks with a ten foot pole.

62371385
>>62371250
Yeah I was considering just going into QQQ and accepting more ups and downs
I don’t know if it’s the best comparison but comparing it’s CAGR to VOO it has either outperformed or tied with VOO recently
Obviously the down years are way worse but again that’s why I am using CAGR as my comparison basis
It seems like this AI shit isn’t going away any time soon leading to something so focused on the top of the market like QQQ seeming more enticing

62371396
>>62369835
Basically I want to be your boyfriend or just glorified cockcleaner while both of our ports build up to 100k (but mine faster than yours but that's okay)

62371737
Also my portfolio currently sits at $61,000
I have a goal of reaching 100k by year end
There’s 174 week days left in the year, ignoring market closed due to holiday
100,000 - 61,000 = 39,000
39,000 / 174 = 224
224 / 61,000 = .003
So
If my math is right
My account only needs to grow .3% per market day to reach 100,000
61,000 * 1.003^174 = 102,728
Am I correct here

62371763
>>62371385
>It seems like this AI shit isn’t going away any time
It's not, this shit is going to fundamentally change most industries in a way that most people cannot even understand. Its primary limitation is just that older generations can't adapt to it, which means it has naysayers now and a clear path of progressive growth over time, similar to computing decades ago.

It's not just AI though honestly. These companies are the future. With population growth leveling out worldwide, there's not going to be a sudden expansion of demand for food, for minerals, for many traditional industries. NASDAQ is better primed for investment in future tech, hence why I prefer it. Or tech ETFs like VGT, but those are less diversified. Just sinking 50/50 into SP500 and NASDAQ might be fine for you to let it ride

62371848
>>62370048
SEX

62372037
>>62371763
i see, thanks for the input

62372074
>>62371737
Heyyy we're port buddies :) YAGMI

62372118
>>62371737
also this fails to recognize buying more shares bringing that rate down significantly

62372136
>>62372074
>>62371737
I lied my port's now at 77k LOL YANGMI

62372167
Due to volatility and human nature its actually mentally easier to go into 5 companies you believe in. keep most money in S&P and add 5

62372242
>>62372136
errrrm ok buddy

62373602
Do we got a chance for 100k by years end fellas

62373605
>hey guys daddy got me a job making 6 figs, please tell me how to be a millionaire
Nepobabies really think life is just people giving them all the tips to succeed, for free, huh. Lol.

62373753
>>62373602
Im all in on nasdaq but yes

item.title: SUFFAH, BEARS! — 11 replies — 3.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
SUFFAH, BEARS!

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-16 03:38 UTC

OP

SUFFAH, BEARS!

Early replies (first 3)
